title: AI Fanvue Page SOP
subtitle: Profile and Content Structure Manual

sections.header: Intro: The Role of the Fanvue Page

blocks.content: The Fanvue page is not a gallery. It is a conversion environment. Its purpose is to turn curiosity into trust and trust into spending.

left.content: The goal is not to show everything. The goal is to suggest everything.

right.content: A good Fanvue page feels like entering a private space rather than browsing a product list. The fan should feel invited into a personal world, not pushed into a checkout decision.

blocks.content: The banner is the first emotional signal. It sets the mood of the fantasy before the fan reads anything. The banner answers one question for the fan: What kind of world am I entering.

items: AI persona in a natural, believable environment
Bedroom, couch, mirror, or window settings
Casual and personal pose, not staged

left.content: Soft persona = relaxed and approachable. Dominant persona = confident and controlled.

right.content:
Natural bedroom selfie, soft lighting,
casual pose, face clearly visible

Oversexualized pose, heavy filter,
staged studio shot, no personality
The banner should suggest lifestyle and mood, not explicit sexuality. It should feel like a private moment rather than an advertisement.

blocks.content: The bio should sound like a person, not a business. It must feel emotional rather than technical. A bad bio focuses on features instead of feelings. Features do not create desire. Feelings create desire.

right.content:
Just a girl who loves late nights and private talks.
I share my daily life and my naughty side here.
Stay and get to know me better.
Daily posts. HD videos. Cheap PPV.
The bio should never look like a shop description. It should look like a short personal introduction.

sections.header: Posts for Free: Trust and Curiosity

blocks.content: Free content is for trust and realism, not for sexual satisfaction. Its job is to make the fan feel safe and curious. Free content should feel like daily life, not porn. It should show personality more than body.

right.title: Critical Rule
right.content: If free content already satisfies desire, the fan has no reason to subscribe.

blocks.content: Subscriber content is where intimacy begins. It must feel like a reward for joining and like something not meant for everyone. The fan should feel he unlocked something meaningful, but not the full fantasy.

left.content: This layer should suggest access without showing everything. It should feel closer, warmer, and more personal than free content.

items: "I would not post this anywhere else"

right.content: Subscriber content should look like it was made for a small private group rather than for the public. Captions should reinforce exclusivity.

blocks.content: PPV content is where the full fantasy is delivered. This is the highest value layer of the page. PPV should never be framed like a product. It must be framed like an emotional event.

right.content:
"I should not have done this earlier"
"I lost control today"
"I do not usually show this"
PPV should feel like a private mistake or secret, not like a catalog item. The fan should feel he is seeing something he was not supposed to see.

blocks.content: Automated messages to free followers are the first emotional touchpoint after a fan follows. Their purpose is not to sell but to make the fan feel noticed and welcome. The goal is to move the fan from passive follower into active chatter.

items: No prices, discounts, or PPV mentions

right.content:
Hey, I just saw you follow me. That was cute
of you. Where are you from.

Thanks for following. Subscribe now for
exclusive content.

blocks.content: Automated subscriber messages are sent after someone subscribes. Their purpose is to emotionally reward the action and guide the fan into deeper engagement. The subscriber must feel that joining unlocked something special and personal.

right.content:
I was hoping you would join me here. This feels
more private already. Tell me what made you
decide to subscribe.
Welcome. Here is my content. Check my PPV.

blocks.content: The subscription price is not designed to generate profit by itself. Its main purpose is to remove fear and make the fan take the first step. The real money is made from chatting and PPV.

items: Income from chatting and PPV = real business

blocks.content: Content must follow a clear ladder. Each layer must leave something unfinished so the next layer becomes attractive.
Free: outfit photo → Subscriber: lingerie photo → PPV: nude video

blocks.title: Critical Rule
blocks.content:
If a fan can get satisfaction for free, he will not subscribe.
If a fan can get full satisfaction from subscription, he will not buy PPV.
All content must look like it comes from the same person. Sudden changes reduce realism and trust.

items: Style and mood should be similar
Hair, makeup, and clothing style should not change drastically

blocks.title: The Chain
blocks.content:
Consistency creates realism.
Realism creates attachment.
Attachment creates spending.

summary_points: The Fanvue page is a conversion environment, not a content gallery
The banner sets the mood — it must match the persona type
The bio must sound human, emotional, and inviting
Free content builds trust, subscriber content builds desire, PPV fulfills fantasy
Each content layer must leave something unfinished
Automated messages create emotional touchpoints, not sales pitches
Subscription price removes fear — real revenue comes from chatting and PPV
Visual consistency protects realism and creates attachment
